hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The string 'Default' is used when the description in the input check is empty or null.

context_pointers.vague_reference: maintain the ordering strategy used in the groups utility
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Preserve the original relative order of all checks that do not belong to any of the newly 'added' groups.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the canonical summary format defined in the helper module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Output exactly: '{group_name}|{method_name}|{true|false}|{description_string}' where true/false reflects the 'enabled' flag.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_contains_matching.json.description: A command is reported as risky when it contains an enabled literal pattern, and non-matching text reports no matched risk.

cases.expected_output:
command[0]=test is valid
match_count=1
match_group=sample
match_description=contains matched
---
command[1]=not-found
match_count=0

feature1_2_prefix_matching.json.description: A command is reported as risky only when it begins with an enabled prefix pattern.

cases.expected_output:
command[0]=test is valid
match_count=1
match_group=sample
match_description=prefix matched
---
command[1]=is
match_count=0

feature1_3_regex_matching.json.description: A command is reported as risky when an enabled regular-expression pattern matches the command text.

cases.expected_output:
command[0]=rm -rf
match_count=1
match_group=sample
match_description=regex matched
---
command[1]=foo
match_count=0

feature2_1_load_configuration.json.description: A YAML configuration is loaded into challenge type, included risk groups, and enabled check summaries.

input.yaml:
---
challenge: Math
includes:
  - default-check
version: 0.2.2
checks:
- from: default-check
  test: default-check
  method: Regex
  enable: true
  description: ""

feature2_2_update_check_groups.json.description: Adding risk groups inserts their default checks without duplicating existing includes, while removing groups deletes their checks from the active configuration.

input.initial_yaml:
---
challenge: Math
includes:
  - default-check
version: 0.2.2
checks:
- from: default-check
  test: default-check
  method: Regex
  enable: true
  description: ""

cases.expected_output:
challenge=Math
includes=default-check,test-1,test-2
check_count=3
check=default-check|Regex|true|Default|
check=test-1|Regex|true|Default|
check=test-2|Regex|true|Default|

input.initial_yaml:
---
challenge: Math
includes:
  - default-check
version: 0.2.2
checks:
- from: default-check
  test: default-check
  method: Regex
  enable: true
  description: ""

feature2_3_reset_configuration.json.description: Resetting configuration replaces the current settings with the default challenge and default included groups, and reports whether a backup was requested.

input.initial_yaml:
---
challenge: Math
includes:
  - default-check
version: 0.2.2
checks:
- from: default-check
  test: default-check
  method: Regex
  enable: true
  description: ""

feature2_4_update_challenge.json.description: Changing the default confirmation challenge preserves the configured checks and includes while replacing the challenge type.

input.initial_yaml:
---
challenge: Math
includes:
  - default-check
version: 0.2.2
checks:
- from: default-check
  test: default-check
  method: Regex
  enable: true
  description: ""

_constraints.input_condition: Feature flag is 'match_commands' with a list of checks and a list of commands
_constraints.output_rule:
For each command, output 'command[N]=...
match_count=[int]
match_group=[string]
match_description=[string]
---'
Report a risk if the check's 'pattern' appears as a substring anywhere in the command text and the check is enabled.
Report a risk only if the command text starts strictly with the check's 'pattern' and the check is enabled.
Report a risk if the check's 'pattern' matches the command text using regex semantics and the check is enabled.

_constraints.input_condition: A check is disabled (enabled=false) or pattern does not match
_constraints.output_rule: Do not output any 'match_group' or 'match_description' lines; 'match_count' must be 0.

_constraints.input_condition: Multiple checks match the same command
_constraints.output_rule: Include a separate output block for each matching check (group description line), maintaining the order of checks or matches.

_constraints.input_condition: Feature flag is 'load_config' with YAML string and an empty output expected
_constraints.output_rule: Output formatted challenge name, included groups (comma joined), total check count, and single check summary line.
The output 'challenge' value must match the YAML 'challenge' value exactly.
Convert the array to a comma-separated string for the 'includes' output line.
Set 'check_count' to 0. Do not output a 'check=' line.

_constraints.input_condition: YAML 'checks' list has exactly one item
_constraints.output_rule: Output one 'check=' line in format: group|method|enabled|description

_constraints.input_condition: Feature flag is 'update_groups' with 'add' and 'remove' lists
_constraints.output_rule: Merge active checks with available checks from added groups, excluding removed groups. Strip duplicates in the 'includes' list.
Remove checks belonging to the specified groups from the active configuration entirely.
Insert checks from the specified groups into the active configuration, preserving order of existing checks outside added groups, then appending added checks.
If 'backup_existing' is true, output 'backup_created=yes' at the very beginning on its own line.
Do not output 'backup_created' line.
Challenge becomes 'Math', includes become 'base,fs,git', and check_count becomes 0.
Replace the 'challenge' value in the output configuration while preserving 'includes' and 'checks'.
The output 'challenge' line must reflect the new value provided in input exactly.
The output must maintain the exact string representation of 'includes' and 'check' lines from the input.

Output lines must be newline-terminated. No trailing whitespace on lines. The file should not end with a newline after the last report line (based on test artifact description), or simply ensure line breaks are single newlines only.

_constraints.input_condition: YAML or input checks have an empty description string
_constraints.output_rule: When parsing/displaying, empty descriptions should likely be treated as 'Default' within the check summary format if the test implies a specific fallback, otherwise raw empty string. Based on C011 example 'Default' appears in output even if input shows empty string, implying a default mapping.

hide.replacement: a fallback string for empty descriptions — verify via test output
hide.oracle_response: The string 'Default' is used when the description in the input check is empty or null.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: maintain the ordering strategy used in the groups utility
vague_refs.actual_meaning: Preserve the original relative order of all checks that do not belong to any of the newly 'added' groups.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: follow the canonical summary format defined in the helper module
vague_refs.actual_meaning: Output exactly: '{group_name}|{method_name}|{true|false}|{description_string}' where true/false reflects the 'enabled' flag.
